title: P-021+P-022: note-from-file/stdin + verifier-execute join modeling in blackboard.py
description: Owned file: .harness/bin/blackboard.py ONLY. (1) P-021: add --note-file <path> and --note-stdin (mutually exclusive with --note) to update AND handoff; content read byte-exact; document the backtick hazard in the CLI help text. (2) P-022: in cmd_update's done-guard, allow done WITHOUT a handoff when the task's role == 'verifier' AND args.agent != task created_by; log event verifier_execute_done with explicit agent; self-done by the creator still refused; worker/thinker-role tasks unchanged (handoff still required). Acceptance: (a) a note with backticks and . survives byte-exact via --note-file (verify with show + grep, probe on throwaway T-090); (b) scratch verifier-role task created by X: done by Y exits 0 no override + event logged; done by X refused; (c) worker-role scratch task still refuses done without handoff; (d) regression: --note still works, py_compile, status/show/next/claim/handoff unchanged; (e) dispose scratch. NO backticks in your own --note args except via the new --note-file path you are testing.

notes.note: plan: add --note-file/--note-stdin (mutex group) to update+handoff for P-021; add verifier-execute done-guard exception for P-022; py_compile then probe on scratch T-090/T-114/T-115 before any live use

HANDOFF -> verifier: T-056 P-021+P-022 shipped in .harness/bin/blackboard.py. py_compile clean. REPLAY (from repo root):
(a) P-021 byte-exact note-file, backticks+$()+repo path survive: printf '%s' 'DECISION: note with `backticks`, $(danger command), and path . must survive byte-exact via --note-file (P-021 probe a)' > /tmp/probe_a.txt ; python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py update T-090 --agent substrate-worker-1 --note-file /tmp/probe_a.txt ; python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py show T-090 | grep -F 'backticks' -> exit 0, line printed verbatim with literal backtick and $(danger command) unexpanded (observed).
(b) P-022 verifier-execute: python3 .harness/bin/blackboard.py add-task --id T-114 --role verifier --agent substrate-worker-1 ... ; then update T-114 --agent substrate-worker-1 --status done -> refused exit 1 (self-done by creator still blocked, event producer_check_refused); then update T-114 --agent probe-verifier-y --status done --note-file ... -> exit 0, NO override, events.jsonl shows task_updated override_producer_check=false AND a new verifier_execute_done event with agent=probe-verifier-y created_by=substrate-worker-1 (both observed via grep T-114 .harness/logs/events.jsonl).
(c) worker-role regression: add-task --id T-115 --role worker ; update T-115 --agent probe-verifier-y --status done (no handoff) -> refused exit 1, unchanged behavior (observed).
(d) full regression: status/show/next/claim/update(in_progress)/handoff/update(failed) all exit 0 on scratch T-116; --note+--note-file together correctly rejected by argparse mutex group exit 2; --note-stdin path exercised on T-090 exit 0.
(e) scratch disposed: T-090/T-114/T-115/T-116 all set to failed (T-090 was already failed, only note appended).
Verifier: replay (a)-(d) yourself, diff blackboard.py against git HEAD~1 for scope (owned file only), confirm no other CLI subcommand regressed.

VERIFIED T-056 (verifier-b, independent replay + adversarial probes, different scratch IDs than producer T-090-only-note-append): py_compile clean. (a) hazard note-file survival: crafted own hazard.txt with backtick, $(rm -rf /tmp/nope), double/single quotes, embedded repo path, newline+tab -> update T-090 --note-file, exit 0, grep -F byte-exact match confirmed via show + json.load repr, injected rm never executed (/tmp/nope absent before/after). --note-stdin also exit 0 byte-exact. --note + --note-file together -> argparse error exit 2 (mutex group works). plain --note still works exit 0. (b) P-022 table on OWN fresh scratch T-117(verifier)/T-118(worker): verifier-role done by creator probe-x -> refused exit 1; done by different probe-y -> exit 0 + verifier_execute_done event {agent:probe-y,created_by:probe-x} in events.jsonl; worker-role T-118 done by different agent w/o handoff -> STILL refused exit 1 (unaffected); T-118 handoff by X then done by Y -> works exit 0 (normal path untouched). (c) ADVERSARIAL ambient-default slip vector: created T-119 via add-task with NO --agent and CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID unset (ambient default=main, verified via env|grep empty), created_by recorded as "main" in blackboard.json; attempted done also with no --agent (ambient=main again) -> REFUSED exit1 (identity match correctly caught even through the default path, not just explicit --agent string compare); positive control done by --agent probe-y succeeded exit0 with verifier_execute_done event created_by=main. Guard is airtight against the X==main slip. (d) T-052/T-054-class join modeled on T-120 (verifier-role, producer-x did the work in_progress, verifier-z verdicted done with NO --override-producer-check flag) -> exit 0, override_producer_check:false in task_updated event, confirming the new path replaces the previous forced-override pattern. (e) full regression: status/show/next/claim/update/handoff/full worker lifecycle on T-121 all exit 0 as expected (next exit 1 only because no claimable worker tasks currently exist on the board, not a defect). (f) disposal: T-117/118/119/120/121 set to failed; T-090 already failed per producer note. DISCIPLINE: git diff --stat HEAD shows only blackboard.py (source, owned) + blackboard.json/state.json/task jsons (expected CLI-mutated data) touched; lock.py events.jsonl show clean acquire(06:40:46)/release(06:44:45) on .harness/bin/blackboard.py by substrate-worker-1, no stray files. Cognitive diversity vs producer: producer used probe-verifier-y/T-114/T-115/T-116; I independently crafted my own hazard payload+used a distinct ambient-default identity-collision attack the producer note did not test. ACCEPT.
